mission.title: external-secrets: PushSecret: option to add additional tags\labels
mission.description: PushSecret: option to add additional tags\labels. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
I would like to be able to manage the tags that are set on the external secret when using PushSecret.

steps.title: Apply the fix for PushSecret: option to add additional tags\labels
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

I've designed the feature to attach metadata to the secrets managed by PushSecrets. The interface is not fixed at all, and I'm open to discussion 🙂 
Relates to https://github.com/external-secrets/external-secrets/issues/1821
Add the `metadata` field to the PushSecret
```yaml
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  refreshInterval: 10s
  secretStoreRefs:
    - name: cluster-secret-store
      kind: ClusterSecretStore
  selector:
    secret:
      name: test-secret
  data:
    - match:
        secretKey: key1
        remoteRef:
          remoteKey: test1
      metadata:
        annotations:
          key1: value1
        labels:
          key1: value1
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "PushSecret: option to add additional tags\labels" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

I've designed the feature to attach metadata to the secrets managed by PushSecrets. The interface is not fixed at all, and I'm open to discussion 🙂 
Relates to https://github.com/external-secrets/external-secrets/issues/1821
Add the `metadata` field to the PushSecret manifest. An example manifest.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: pushsecret-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  refreshInterval: 10s
  secretStoreRefs:
    - name: cluster-secret-store
      kind: ClusterSecretStore
  selector:
    secret:
      name: test-secret
  data:
    - match:
        secretKey: key1
        remoteRef:
          remoteKey: test1
      metadata:
        annotations:
          key1: value1
        labels:
          key1: value1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
